title: Brute Force TSP
code:
BRUTE_FORCE_TSP(distMatrix, n):
 minCost = INFINITY
 bestTour = []
 cities = [1, 2, ..., n-1] // excluding start city 0
 
 FOR each permutation P of cities:
 tour = [0] + P + [0] // start and return to start
 cost = 0
 FOR i = 0 to n-1:
 cost = cost + distMatrix[tour[i]][tour[i+1]]
 IF cost < minCost:
 minCost = cost
 bestTour = tour
 
 RETURN minCost, bestTour
explanation: Iterates through all (n-1)! permutations, computes total distance for each tour including return to start, returns minimum. Guaranteed optimal but O(n!) time.

title: Held-Karp Dynamic Programming TSP
description: DP approach with exponential improvement over brute force
code:
HELD_KARP_TSP(distMatrix, n):
 // DP[mask][i] = min cost to visit cities in mask ending at i
 DP[2^n][n]
 
 // Base case: visit single city (mask with only bit i set)
 FOR i = 1 to n-1:
 DP[1<<i][i] = distMatrix[0][i]
 
 // Fill DP table for subsets of increasing size
 FOR mask FROM 1 TO 2^n - 1:
 FOR last FROM 1 TO n-1:
 IF bit NOT set in mask: CONTINUE
 prevMask = mask WITHOUT bit last
 IF prevMask == 0: CONTINUE
 
 DP[mask][last] = min over j in prevMask:
 DP[prevMask][j] + distMatrix[j][last]
 
 // Complete tour by returning to start
 fullMask = (1<<n) - 1
 minCost = min over i FROM 1 TO n-1:
 DP[fullMask][i] + distMatrix[i][0]
 
 RETURN minCost
explanation: Builds optimal solutions for subsets incrementally. Time complexity O(n²×2^n) - much better than n! for reasonable n.

title: Nearest Neighbor Heuristic
code:
NEAREST_NEIGHBOR_TSP(distMatrix, n, start):
 visited[0..n-1] = FALSE
 tour = [start]
 current = start
 visited[start] = TRUE
 
 FOR step = 1 TO n-1:
 nearest = -1
 minDist = INFINITY
 
 FOR j = 0 TO n-1:
 IF NOT visited[j] AND distMatrix[current][j] < minDist:
 minDist = distMatrix[current][j]
 nearest = j
 
 tour.append(nearest)
 visited[nearest] = TRUE
 current = nearest
 
 tour.append(start) // return to start
 cost = calculateTotalDistance(tour, distMatrix)
 
 RETURN cost, tour
explanation: Greedy algorithm always picks nearest unvisited city. Fast O(n²) but not optimal. Good for getting initial solution for improvement algorithms.

title: 2-opt Local Search Improvement
description: Improves existing tour by edge swaps
code:
2OPT_IMPROVE(tour, distMatrix, n):
 improved = TRUE
 
 WHILE improved:
 improved = FALSE
 FOR i = 1 TO n-2:
 FOR j = i+1 TO n-1:
 // Remove edges (i-1,i) and (j,j+1)
 // Add edges (i-1,j) and (i,j+1)
 delta = -distMatrix[tour[i-1]][tour[i]] 
 - distMatrix[tour[j]][tour[j+1]]
 + distMatrix[tour[i-1]][tour[j]]
 + distMatrix[tour[i]][tour[j+1]]
 
 IF delta < 0:
 // Reverse segment between i and j
 reverse tour from i to j
 improved = TRUE
 
 RETURN tour
explanation: Iteratively improves tour by removing two edges and reconnecting in a better way. Runs until no improvement possible - finds local optimum.
